content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

June 26, 1991

Stella Matthews  
Registrar of Voters Office  
Campaign Reporting Section  
P.O. Box 30450  
Los Angeles, CA 90030

Re: Combined Campaign Report  
Our File No. I-91-312

Dear Ms. Matthews:

This is to confirm advice provided to you on June 19, 1991, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ This letter constitutes written advice under Section 84205.

Section 84205 states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this title.

A special run-off election has been called for Assembly District 46. The run-off election date is July 30, 1991. Because the period covered by the pre-run-off election statement due in connection with the July 30, 1991, election (Section 84200.8) extends beyond the closing date for the semi-annual statement due for the first six months of 1991 (Section 84200), we believe it is appropriate for candidates being voted on in that election, their controlled committees, and committees primarily formed to support or oppose candidates in that election to combine those two reports.

Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on the following schedule:

1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Stella Matthews

Type of Statement | Period Covered | Filing Deadline
Pre-run-off Election Combined with semi-annual statement for the first half of 1991 | May 19 - July 13 | July 18

The contribution limitations imposed by Proposition 73 are still in effect for special elections held to fill a vacant elective office. A contributor may contribute up to the stated limits (see enclosure) in the special run-off election cycle, less any amount the candidate transfers to his or her special election committee bank account from any other campaign account which is attributable to that contributor.

"Special run-off election cycle" means the day after the special election until the day of the special run-off election.

If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

by: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

cc: Secretary of State
